stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is between 62% and 74% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is 82%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is 74%. What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.376 to 0.449 as the departmental review pass rate varies from 62% to 74%. Point estimate: 0.413.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the departmental review stage creates a 7.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the departmental review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
